title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Francistown Town Council Roads and Traffic Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Francistown Town Council Roads and Traffic Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-Laws may be cited as the Francistown Town Council Roads and Traffic Bye-Laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content:
2. Application
These Bye-Laws shall apply to
( a ) the municipal area of Francistown; and
( b ) land owned or governed by the municipality but outside the limits of the municipal area of Francistown.

provisions.title: Interpretation

definitions.definition: any person authorised by the Council for the purpose of giving effect to these bye-laws

a place on an omnibus route intended for the picking up and depositing of passengers, which may be demarcated by yellow lines and indicated by a sign bearing the words "Bus Stop" or the word "Bus" painted in yellow on the road surface within such bus stop
that portion of a road formed for the use of vehicular traffic and includes that portion of a road between kerbs or temporary kerbs
driving a motor vehicle forward into a parking bay
a vehicle propelled by mechanical power and specially designed, constructed or adapted for the use of persons suffering from some physical defect or disability
repair and renewal, and maintain shall be constructed accordingly
a parking bay whereon the parking of motor vehicles is controlled and regulated by a parking meter
any land which is provided and maintained by the Council as a place for the parking of motor vehicles, whether or not a charge is levied for parking

an area in any road which is designated by means of
( a ) a sign or signs erected or placed so as to indicate the extremities of the area and bearing the words "No Parking"
a subdivision of a car park or of a parking area intended for the accommodation of a single vehicle

a mechanical device for the purpose of controlling and regulating the parking of motor vehicles in parking bays or in parking areas where such meters are installed, and includes the standard to which the meter is affixed
